title: Mainnet launch readiness checklist
description:
## Context
MAINNET_DEPLOYMENT_RUNBOOK.md exists but there is no tracking bead.
This is the master checklist for mainnet launch.

## Key Blockers
- [ ] v1.1.0 deployed to all fleet nodes
- [ ] Solo→AuxPoW transition hardened (SOQ-INFRA-021)
- [ ] Privacy RPCs resolved (SOQ-INFRA-019)
- [ ] SOQUPOOL production pool verified on mainnet config
- [ ] DNS seeds pointing to mainnet nodes
- [ ] SoquShield mainnet network verified

## Reference
- docs/MAINNET_DEPLOYMENT_RUNBOOK.md
- security/SECURITY_ISSUE_REGISTRY.md (open items)

title: USDSOQ wallet-to-wallet sends (SendUSDSOQ)
description:
## Context
soq-signer has MintUSDSOQ() for authority mints via bridge, but SendUSDSOQ()
for wallet-to-wallet transfers does NOT exist yet. SoquShield send screen
shows 'Coming Soon' for USDSOQ sends.

## Files
- soq-signer: needs SendUSDSOQ() function
- soqushield/lib/screens/send/send_screen.dart: remove 'Coming Soon' gate
- soq-signer/internal/txbuilder/builder.go: USDSOQ output construction

## Acceptance
- [ ] SendUSDSOQ() implemented in soq-signer
- [ ] SoquShield send screen allows USDSOQ sends
- [ ] E2E test: mint USDSOQ → send to another address → verify balance

## Reference
- SYSTEMS_ENGINEERING_ONBOARDING.md §7.6 (documents the gap)

title: L2SOQ/eltoo E2E testing
description:
## Context
Wire Lightning to L1 eltoo. APO (SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT) at witness v6.
## Files
- src/script/interpreter.cpp (APO, CSFS, CTV)
- src/test/covenant_tests.cpp
## Acceptance
- [ ] Open payment channel on stagenet
- [ ] Bidirectional payments
- [ ] Cooperative close
- [ ] Unilateral close (timeout)

title: SoquShield audit: 50+ findings
description: Fix Halborn SSC audit findings in wallet code

comments.author: buddy
comments.text:
## Status Update (June 3, 2026)

This tracks the Halborn SSC (Smart Contract / Source Code) audit findings.
SOQ-H001 through SOQ-H030 are all remediated in the main codebase.

The '50+ findings' are the SoquShield (Flutter/Dart) wallet-specific items
that were identified during the same audit cycle. These are separate from
the C++ core findings.

Reference: soqucoin-ops/security/assessments/halborn-mainnet/

title: v1.1.0 binary build and fleet deploy
description:
## Context
v1.1.0 is tagged and pushed (94ebd7e79) but the binary has NOT been built or deployed.
This release fixes 2 critical + 3 high USDSOQ consensus bugs.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Build soqucoind on Mining VPS (make -j2)
- [ ] SCP binary to all fleet nodes (Services, Broadcast, Monitoring)
- [ ] Restart nodes in sequence (Mining → Broadcast → Services → Monitoring)
- [ ] Verify all nodes report v1.1.0 via getnetworkinfo
- [ ] Run 503 test suite on at least one node

## Reference
- doc/release-notes/release-notes-1.1.0.md
- CHANGELOG.md [1.1.0] entry

title: UBS remediation: 687 critical findings
description: Triage and fix critical findings from UBS scan. Tabled until after flywheel phases.

comments.author: buddy
comments.text:
## Status Update (June 3, 2026)

The 'UBS' scan refers to ASAN/UBSAN sanitizer testing (Jan 26, 2026).
The original scan found memory errors and UB across the Soqucoin codebase.

**ASAN/UBSAN scan itself: ✅ ALL CLEAR** (Jan 26 — 0 ASan errors, 0 UBSan critical)
- Fixed: Binius64 shift overflow (commit 6660fc71e)
- Fixed: CFeeRate integer overflow (commit 9c5144653)
- 263 tests pass under sanitizers

What the bead ACTUALLY tracks is the **broader codebase audit** that spawned from this:
- SOQ-H001 through SOQ-H030: 30 Halborn findings → ALL 30 REMEDIATED
- SOQ-INFRA-017 through SOQ-INFRA-027: 11 infra findings → ~6 remediated, ~5 open
- SOQ-D001, SOQ-D003: 2 crypto findings → 1 remediated, 1 deferred

The '687 critical findings' number likely refers to the total static analysis + dynamic scan output before triage and dedup. After triage: ~43 unique issues tracked in SECURITY_ISSUE_REGISTRY.md.

**Remaining open items tracked in registry:**
- SOQ-INFRA-017: soq-address-watcher RPC saturation (service disabled)
- SOQ-INFRA-019: disablewallet=1 blocks privacy RPCs
- SOQ-INFRA-020: test binary link failure (partial fix)
- SOQ-INFRA-021: RC5 solo→AuxPoW transition hardening
- SOQ-INFRA-027: soq-signer static fee_rate
- SOQ-D003: CT amount overflow review (deferred to Stage 3)

Reference: soqucoin-ops/security/SECURITY_ISSUE_REGISTRY.md

## Acceptance

- [ ] LatticeFold accumulator updates
- [ ] Key-image prevents double-spend

- src/script/interpreter.cpp
- src/test/usdsoq_tests.cpp
## Acceptance

- [ ] Transfer between addresses
- [ ] Burn and verify supply tracking
- [ ] Freeze/unfreeze addresses

title: Privacy RPCs: disablewallet=1 blocks shielding (SOQ-INFRA-019)
description:
## Context
Services VPS runs disablewallet=1 (eliminates wallet mutex contention) but
this disables ALL privacy RPCs: getprivacyinfo, shieldsoq, unshieldsoq, etc.

## Resolution Options
A) Dedicated privacy node (~1 day)
B) Client-side proof generation via liblatticebp FFI (~2-3 weeks)
C) Re-enable wallet after UTXO indexer (SOQ-INFRA-017 prerequisite)

## Reference
- security/SECURITY_ISSUE_REGISTRY.md SOQ-INFRA-019

title: Remove secp256k1 dead code — 345 files, 43MB of classical crypto never called
description:
## Context
secp256k1 (ECDSA elliptic curve library) is compiled and linked into every Soqucoin binary
but has ZERO active function calls. All crypto uses ML-DSA-44 (Dilithium).
Ian Smith (QuantumEVM/HSA) publicly flagged this in a tweet listing Soqucoin as 'not safe'.

## Evidence
- 0 #include statements of secp256k1 headers in our source
- 0 function calls to secp256k1_* in consensus/wallet/RPC code
- CKey::MakeNewKey() → pure Dilithium
- CPubKey::Verify() → pure Dilithium
- 345 files, 43MB, still compiled and linked

## Acceptance
- [ ] src/secp256k1/ deleted (git rm -rf)
- [ ] Build system cleaned (Makefile.am, configure.ac, autogen.sh)
- [ ] All stale ECDSA/secp256k1 comments updated
- [ ] Test scripts updated (remove DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH refs)
- [ ] Full build succeeds (make clean && make)
- [ ] 503 test cases pass
- [ ] PQCAT-style scan shows zero classical crypto in consensus
- [ ] Binary no longer contains secp256k1 symbols (nm check)
- [ ] Security registry entry added (SOQ-SECP-001)
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry added

## Reference
- Implementation plan in conversation artifacts
- Ian Smith tweet: @IanSmith_HSA June 3, 2026
- SOQ-INFRA-016 (Bulletproofs deprecation, already done)
close_reason: Removed: 259 files, -116,610 lines, -43MB. Commit 76236acc9. PQCAT scan clean.
